titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alvin Novick Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michelle Peralta

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 14, 2024 at 07:00

did.abstract:
The papers primarily document Novick's research and advocacy work related to AIDS prevention, including work with various LGBT and AIDS organizations, the New Haven Mayor's Task Force on AIDS, and AIDS Project New Haven, as well as national, state, and local agencies related to public policy and treatment of AIDS.
Also included is a selection of Novick's research on bat echolocation, teaching and administrative materials as a Yale faculty member and residential fellow of Jonathan Edwards College, and personal correspondence and photographs.
Materials in the papers include correspondence with medical professionals, government officials, and federal health agencies, statistical reports, grant applications, reprints and unpublished manuscripts by Novick, as well as medical and LGBT newsletters and publications.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Estate of Alvin Novick, 2018.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Alvin Novick was born on June 27, 1925 in Flushing, New York.

During World War II Novick served in the United States Army and was a prisoner of war for three months after being captured in the Battle of the Bulge. After the war, Novick attended Harvard College, earning a degree in biochemical sciences in 1947 before earning a doctorate in medicine in 1951 from Harvard Medical School.
After working as a physician for a few years, Novick pivoted to researching echolocation systems of bats. He published extensively on bats, including the book The World of Bats .

In 1957 Novick joined the faculty of Yale University as an assistant professor in the Department of Zoology, eventually becoming an associate professor and then professor of biology in 1983. Novick was also a fellow at Jonathan Edwards College, one of Yale University's residential colleges.

In the early 1980s, in the midst of the growing AIDS crisis, Novick began to advocate of for AIDS education, awareness, and ethical public policy. He was the President for the American Association of Physicians for Human Rights, now the Gay and Lesbian Medical Association, as well as the co-founder of AIDS Project New Haven and Leeway, a nursing facility dedicated to treating people with AIDS.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers primarily document Alvin Novick's research and advocacy work related to AIDS prevention, particularly work with various LGBT and AIDS organizations, the New Haven Mator's Task Force on AIDS, and the AIDS Project New Haven.

Novick's involvement with national, state, and local organizations related to public policy and awareness of the AIDS treatment and prevention is also well documented, as is Novick's research on medical school education related to human sexuality.

Also included is a selection of Novick's research on bat echolocation, administrative materials as a Yale faculty member and residential fellow of Jonathan Edwards College, and personal correspondence and photographs.

Materials in the papers include correpondence with medical professionals, government officials, and federal health agencies, statistical reports, grant applications, reprints and unpublished manuscripts by Novick, and medical and LGBT newsletters and publications.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alvin Novick Papers (MS 2087). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale Univeristy Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processing staff arranged and described these materials according to national and local standards. Some material, including the medical school questionnaire on human sexuality courses (Box 3), had been severely damaged by water prior to acquistion. Preservation photocopies were made and the originals destroyed due to their condition. Processing staff have also removed some publications and duplicate materials from these materials.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Alvin Novick was transferred to Yale University in 2018. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged by topic and therein chronologically.

did.unittitle: AIDS Information for the General Community
Risk Reduction for IV Drug Users
"What Every Prostitute Should Know About AIDS"
Affection and Sex for Persons with AIDS or AIDS Related Illnesses
Reduccion de Reiesgo para Usarios de Drogas Intravenousas (IV)/ Reduction and Risk for Intraveneous (IV) Drug Users
Questionnaire on human sexuality education in medical school
American Association of Physicians for Human Rights (AAPHR)
Lesbian and Gay organizations - Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Conneticut Association of Physicians for Human Rights (CAPHR)
National Lesbian and Gay Health Foundation (NLGHF)
Lambda Legal Defense and Education Fund
Public Responsibility in Medicine and Research (PRIM&R)
